items.title: Launch universal macOS DMG for all Macs in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a universal macOS DMG that supports both Intel and Apple Silicon Macs, allowing for a seamless installation experience. The DMG is built to include both x64 and arm64 architectures, ensuring compatibility across different hardware. Key modifications were made to the electron-builder configuration to facilitate this change.

items.title: Enhance Zhihu Connector with Required Skills in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the `required_skills: [acedatacloud/cn-blog]` to the Zhihu connector, enabling it to route correctly within the aichat2 worker. Additionally, the description has been updated to clarify that users can now read their article lists and interaction data. The changes ensure better functionality and integration with the new skill.

items.title: Enhance BYOC Credential Handling for Cookie Skills in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the handling of BYOC credentials by enabling cookie jars to be injected as JSON, allowing skills to access complete cookie data. The change ensures that existing string-based credentials remain unaffected while broadening the type definition for credentials to accommodate non-string values. Additionally, domain-specific cookie handling has been refined to enhance security.

items.title: Empower Your Zhihu Experience with BYOC Skill in Skills 🚀
items.summary: Introducing the new 'cn-blog' skill that allows users to read and publish content on Zhihu using their own login cookies. This feature fills a gap by enabling users to retrieve their published articles along with engagement stats, a first for our automation tools. The skill operates solely with standard libraries, ensuring compatibility without requiring sandbox modifications.

items.title: Introducing Cloud Pull-Mode Server for Digital Human in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the first CI-deployable artifacts for the digital-human pull architecture, featuring a lightweight FastAPI cloud server. The server accepts requests at `/digital-human/*`, enqueues tasks to a shared Mongo queue, and runs a billing monitor, while remaining dormant and not affecting live traffic. Key files added include deployment and service configurations, a Dockerfile, and a docker-compose setup.

items.title: Fix electron-builder channel configuration in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update corrects the `publish.channel` configuration for electron-builder, ensuring it is properly nested under `publish` instead of the root. The build steps have all succeeded, resolving the previous misplacement of the CLI override. This change enhances the reliability of the auto-build process for desktop applications.

items.title: Automated Installer Builds on Every Merge in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces an automated build process for Windows `.exe` and macOS `.dmg` installers on every merge to the main branch. The workflow is restructured to ensure that builds occur without the need for approval, while the publish step remains gated for admin review. This change enhances the development workflow by providing immediate access to beta artifacts after each merge.

items.title: Enhance Desktop OS Detection for Windows and macOS in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces runtime detection for Windows and macOS in the Nexior desktop application. Key changes include the addition of `getDesktopOS()`, `isWindows()`, and `isMacOS()` functions, allowing for independent version upgrades and tailored user experiences. The backend has been updated to support per-OS versioning, ensuring a seamless upgrade process for users on different platforms.

items.title: Enhance OS-specific version gating for Nexior in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces OS-specific version gating for the Nexior desktop client, allowing independent force-upgrades for Windows and macOS. The version gate is inert by default, ensuring a fail-safe mechanism until explicitly set. Key files modified include `app_version.py` and `settings.py`, enhancing the overall configuration for desktop clients.

items.title: Enhanced version control for Nexior desktop client in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces support for the Nexior desktop platform by adding a new entry to the app version gate. The desktop version is inert by default, ensuring it won't block until an operational decision is made to raise the minimum supported version. Additionally, the store URL for the desktop client has been configured, and documentation has been updated accordingly.

items.title: Updated GPU Performance Insights in Platform Service 🤖
items.summary: This update refines the GPU performance story for the native-WSL2 stack, revealing that real rendering speeds are capture-bound rather than shader-bound. Key findings include a maximum speedup of 1.3× for certain workloads, emphasizing the importance of CPU worker capacity and NVENC over GPU shading advantages. The documentation has been updated to reflect these insights and supersedes previous framing.

items.title: Expanded Telegram Skill with 18 Commands in Skills 🚀
items.summary: The Telegram skill has been significantly enhanced, expanding from 5 to 18 commands, including new features like search-global, contacts, and chat-info. All commands have been end-to-end verified against a real account, ensuring robust functionality for reading and writing messages. Key improvements also include better error handling and command parsing adjustments.

items.title: Fix desktop build for Electron in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where the `build:electron` command was not correctly applying the `VITE_SURFACE=desktop` environment variable to the `vite build` process. As a result, the output was incorrectly directed to `dist/` instead of `dist-electron/`, and the bundle was built in web mode, rendering the desktop surface inactive. The fix ensures that both commands in the build process utilize the correct environment variable, restoring functionality for the desktop surface.

items.title: Enhance Discord Bot Integration with BYOC Connector in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new BYOC connector for Discord bots, allowing users to send and read messages using their bot token. The connector is designed to comply with Discord's terms of service by ensuring that personal accounts are not automated. Key features include a hyphen-free namespace and seamless integration with existing skills, enhancing the overall functionality of the platform.

items.title: Enhance Discord Bot Capabilities in Skills 🤖
items.summary: This update introduces the `acedatacloud/discordbot` skill, enabling a connected Discord Bot to send and read messages via the Discord REST API. Key features include listing guild channels, reading recent messages, and sending replies, all while ensuring compliance with Discord's permissions and intents. The implementation has been thoroughly reviewed and confirmed to be secure and functional.

items.title: Streamlined Desktop CI with Unsigned Beta and Notarization in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update enhances the desktop CI pipeline by allowing unsigned beta builds when signing certificates are absent, facilitating immediate testing. Additionally, macOS notarization has been revamped to utilize the App Store Connect API key, eliminating the need for Apple ID credentials. The changes streamline the release process while maintaining security and efficiency.

items.title: Enhance desktop login flow with postMessage in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the email/password login process for the Nexior desktop client by utilizing postMessage instead of deep linking, which was previously blocked in the app's iframe. The changes ensure that the token is passed directly to the parent without requiring a deep link or state, while mobile functionality remains unchanged. The code has been verified with no new TypeScript errors and a clean ESLint output.

items.title: Hide Dead Reddit & LinkedIn Connectors in Backend 🚫
items.summary: This update hides the Reddit and LinkedIn connectors from the catalog due to the absence of production OAuth credentials. The changes include modifying the sync_connectors script to support a hidden flag and updating the connectors.yaml file to mark these entries as hidden. This ensures users only see functional connectors, improving the overall user experience.

items.title: Enhance API with Per-Call Cost Reporting in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a new design for including per-call cost in API responses, addressing requests from three partners: Covenant, Hatcher, and Xona. A read-only `/price` endpoint is added to ensure accurate billing without altering existing deduction logic. This change allows partners to record real costs in their settlement ledgers, enhancing transparency and accuracy in billing.

items.title: Enhance Discord Connector Functionality in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates the Discord connector with the required skills for the acedatacloud/discord skill. It ensures that the aichat connectorsBlock correctly routes to the Discord skill, preventing errors related to missing MCP servers. Additionally, connecting Discord will now automatically install the necessary skill, streamlining the user experience.

items.title: Inject Discord OAuth Token for Enhanced Skill Functionality in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update maps the Discord provider to the DISCORD_TOKEN in the PROVIDER_ENV_VAR, allowing the aichat2 worker to authenticate user requests effectively. The change ensures that the connected user's Discord bearer token is utilized, enabling secure skill interactions without token leakage. This is part of a coordinated effort across multiple repositories to enhance skill capabilities.

items.title: Enhance Discord Integration with New Skill in Skills 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces the `acedatacloud/discord` skill, enabling users to access their Discord identity and list of guilds through the aichat interface. The skill utilizes the Discord API securely, ensuring that only authorized endpoints are accessed. This addition is part of a coordinated effort to enhance user interactions with Discord within the platform.

items.title: Proven GPU Acceleration for WSL2 Chromium in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update documents the successful benchmarking of the WSL2 Chromium-GPU path using a native WSL2 Ubuntu setup on an RTX 3090. Key findings show a significant performance improvement, achieving approximately 63× faster rendering for shader-heavy WebGL compared to software rendering. The requirements for enabling GPU access in the worker container are now clearly outlined, correcting previous assumptions and ensuring accurate implementation.

items.title: Introducing Telegram BYOC Connector for Seamless Messaging in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update adds the Telegram BYOC connector, enabling users to connect their personal Telegram accounts using `api_id`, `api_hash`, and `session_string`, securely stored with AES-256-GCM encryption. The connector allows for various messaging operations, enhancing the capabilities of the aichat2 sandbox with verified environment variable injections. This integration pairs with the Telegram skill and PlatformService for a robust messaging experience.

items.title: Enhance Telegram Skill with Telethon Preinstallation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update preinstalls `telethon==1.42.0` in the skill sandbox image, enabling the `telegram` skill to operate using MTProto. The decision to preinstall is due to the non-root nature of the sandbox, ensuring reliable functionality without runtime pip-install. This change supports the ongoing development of the Telegram connector.

items.title: Enhance Captions and Voice Chip Functionality in Nexior 🤖
items.summary: This update addresses two key issues in the voice-call screen. Captions are now readable in both light and dark themes by ensuring a dark background for the caption text. Additionally, the voice picker chip will always display a voice name, even if the selected voice is falsy, improving user experience.

items.title: Introducing Telegram Skill for Personal Messaging in Skills 🚀
items.summary: This update adds a new `telegram` skill that allows users to interact with their personal Telegram accounts via MTProto using the Telethon library. Key commands include `whoami`, `list-chats`, `get-messages`, `search`, and `send`, all of which have been end-to-end verified against a live account. Improvements include runtime gating for sending messages and ensuring session-local file handling to prevent race conditions.

items.title: Integrate Discord OAuth Credentials into Auth Backend in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update wires the Discord OAuth client ID and secret into the auth-backend deployment, enabling the Discord OAuth connector to function properly. Key changes include modifications to the deployment YAML and the creation script for the new K8s secret. This ensures that the necessary environment variables are set, allowing for seamless integration with Discord's OAuth service.

items.title: Enhance Skill Connection Validation in Backend 🚀
items.summary: This update fixes two critical issues in the `validate_skill_provider_refs` command. It now accurately validates connection references against both OAuth providers and catalog connector identifiers, eliminating false positives. Additionally, it corrects a model reference error that caused crashes, ensuring the command functions smoothly and reliably.

items.title: Enhance OAuth Error Messaging in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the OAuth callback page by providing specific error messages from the provider, allowing users to understand the exact reason for connection failures. It distinguishes between provider-rejected requests and missing parameters, enhancing user experience. Additionally, a button has been added to redirect users back to the connections page.

items.title: Enhance Connector Add Button with Tooltip in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the user experience by adding a tooltip to the Add button for connectors that are not installable. When a connector is disabled, users will now see a 'Coming Soon' message, clarifying its status. The implementation maintains the existing layout and reuses the translated string across all locales.

items.title: Enhance BYOC Credential Flow in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update improves the deep-link installation process for BYOC connectors by rendering the credential form directly on the install page. Users can now enter their credentials inline instead of being redirected, enhancing the experience for connectors like Tencent Cloud and WeChat. The changes ensure that the existing OAuth and error handling paths remain unaffected, maintaining system stability.

items.title: Enhanced GPU Box Functionality in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update verifies the end-to-end functionality of the GPU box on a 3090, confirming successful task execution and CDN uploads. Key improvements include GPU compute capabilities and a build-local fallback option. Documentation has been updated to reflect these changes and clarify GPU usage under Docker Desktop.

items.title: Add Discord OAuth2 Connector for User Identity and Guilds in Backend 🎉
items.summary: This update introduces a new Discord OAuth2 connector, allowing users to authenticate and retrieve their Discord identity, email, and list of guilds. Key files modified include the addition of the `DiscordAdapter`, updates to the registry, and new tests to ensure functionality. The integration supports seamless user experience while maintaining security and privacy standards.

items.title: Introducing the Telegram Connector Design in Index 🚀
items.summary: We've added a comprehensive design for a new Telegram connector, benchmarked against the Google connector. This document outlines key features such as session management, skill integration, and a phased implementation approach. The design has undergone an adversarial review, ensuring all claims are verified and adjustments made for a robust solution.

items.title: Prevent Double Billing on Historical Tasks in Platform Service 🚫
items.summary: This update introduces a safeguard to the billing monitor, ensuring it only bills tasks completed after a designated cutover time. By implementing a `BILLING_CUTOVER` guard, tasks finished before this time will be skipped, preventing any double billing of historical tasks. The changes affect several files, including persister and task store, and include necessary tests to verify the new behavior.

items.title: Enhance DeepSeek Tool Calls with Reasoning Content in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update ensures that every tool-calling assistant message in DeepSeek carries non-empty `reasoning_content`, addressing the previous issue of flaky 400 errors during multi-tool calls. The fix involves adding a placeholder for cases without captured reasoning, ensuring consistent responses across all tool interactions. Comprehensive testing confirms that all scenarios now return successful results.

items.title: Improve error messaging for video failures in Platform Service 🎥
items.summary: This update enhances the error messaging for failed video tasks in the cqtai module. It now includes the upstream error message in the response, allowing users to understand the specific reason for the failure instead of receiving a generic 'unknown error' message. This change applies to both Veo and Sora2 tasks, improving clarity and reducing the need for manual log investigations.

items.title: Refactor billing to server-side for efficiency in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update moves the billing process from the worker pod to a dedicated server-side monitor, enhancing efficiency and eliminating the need for a gateway in the worker pod. Key changes include modifications to server.py and main.py, ensuring that the worker only interacts with MongoDB. The billing semantics remain unchanged, ensuring a smooth transition without impacting existing consumer contracts.

items.title: Fix voice connection and UI issues in Nexior 🎤
items.summary: This update addresses critical issues with voice calls in the Nexior application. The voice picker has been relocated to avoid interference with the wallet display, and a new WebSocket query parameter for voice has been implemented to resolve call errors. Additionally, mid-call voice changes are now handled more safely to prevent disruptions.

items.title: Fix voice parameter handling for realtime calls in Platform Service 🎤
items.summary: This update resolves a critical issue where realtime voice calls failed due to a missing 'session.type' parameter. The fix includes validating the voice parameter from the query string and ensuring it is correctly nested in the session update. Additionally, the session sanitizer now injects the 'session.type' to maintain compatibility with the GA structure, ensuring all updates are valid.

items.title: Fix tool-continuation issues in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update addresses multiple causes of 400 errors in aichat2 conversations, specifically related to assistant message prefill and DeepSeek reasoning content. Key fixes include ensuring that trailing prose is correctly folded into assistant messages and persisting reasoning content for DeepSeek models. These changes enhance the reliability of tool-continuation interactions and improve overall user experience.

items.title: Desktop Client Implementation Status and Next Steps in Index 🚀
items.summary: The README for the Nexior desktop client has been updated to reflect the completed implementation and merged changes. Key updates include a detailed implementation status table and a list of remaining tasks such as CI certs, beta rehearsals, and design verifications. This progress enhances the overall functionality and readiness of the desktop application for users.

items.title: Enhance Native Redirect Flow for Desktop in Frontend Web 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a centralized native deep-link flow for the Nexior desktop client, ensuring secure OAuth callbacks by echoing the `state` parameter. Key changes include the addition of a new utility for managing deep links and updates to several Vue components to utilize this functionality. The implementation maintains backward compatibility for mobile users while enhancing security against potential phishing attacks.

items.title: Fix voice call issues and enhance wallet selection in Nexior 🎉
items.summary: This update addresses four key bugs in the Realtime voice-call screen, including fixing the orb clipping issue in Safari, resolving a stale cancel error, and improving the wallet app selection process. A new voice picker has also been added, allowing users to select their preferred voice during calls. These changes enhance the overall user experience and functionality of the application.

items.title: Enhance Automatic Adversarial Review for All Changes in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update makes the automatic adversarial review unconditional, ensuring it runs before every PR and plan finalization without exceptions. Key documentation files have been updated to reflect this change, enhancing clarity and usability for authors. The new process is efficient, allowing trivial changes to self-converge quickly, thus minimizing overhead.

items.title: Upgrade actions/checkout to v7 in x402 🚀
items.summary: This update bumps the actions/checkout dependency from version 4 to version 7 across multiple workflow files. Key changes include improved handling for fork PRs and various dependency updates, enhancing overall functionality and security. This upgrade ensures better compatibility and performance for CI/CD processes.

items.title: Revamping Digital Human to Pull Mode in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refactors the digital-human system from a push model to a pull model, integrating it with the maestro on a unified GPU queue. Key additions include a new MongoDB-backed task store, a FastAPI cloud server, and a dedicated pull worker for GPU tasks. The changes are additive and dormant, ensuring no disruption to existing production behavior while enhancing future scalability.

items.title: Transform CLAUDE.md into a Real File for Better Compatibility in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update replaces the symlink of `CLAUDE.md` with a regular file that imports `@AGENTS.md`, ensuring cross-platform compatibility. The change eliminates issues with Windows checkouts where symlinks are not supported, preserving the single source of truth in `AGENTS.md`. This adjustment enhances accessibility and maintains the integrity of project documentation.

items.title: Improve Error Classification for Task Creation in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update refines the error handling for the `createTask` function in the cqtai module. It now correctly classifies upstream errors, reducing false 5xx alerts by mapping specific error codes to appropriate HTTP responses. The changes enhance clarity for clients and improve logging for better traceability.

items.title: Enhance Maestro with GPU Lock and Watchdog in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates GPU-lock functionality for the maestro worker, ensuring exclusive access to a shared GPU box while preventing double occupancy. A standalone watchdog has been added to manage stuck jobs, and deployment configurations have been updated for seamless operation. The changes maintain existing cloud behavior while enabling enhanced GPU capabilities on local setups.

items.title: Enhance Maestro with Owner-Fence and Billing Improvements in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update integrates owner-fence concurrency control and a poison budget mechanism into the live maestro worker, ensuring that stale workers do not execute late writes. Additionally, it implements owner-guarded billing to prevent double charges, enhancing the reliability of the billing process. Comprehensive tests have been added to validate these changes, ensuring a safer rollout.

items.title: Streamlined Desktop Release Pipeline in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR finalizes the desktop release pipeline for Nexior, incorporating build, sign, notarize, and publish processes for both Windows and macOS. Key updates include the addition of E2E smoke tests, automated artifact uploads to Tencent Cloud COS, and enhanced documentation for release procedures. The workflow now requires admin approval before any release, ensuring a robust deployment process.

items.title: Exciting Electron Shell Features Added in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR introduces significant enhancements to the Nexior desktop client, focusing on the Electron main process. Key updates include a custom protocol for serving the SPA, OAuth deep-link handling, and an in-memory state nonce store for secure authentication. Additionally, the auto-updater has been integrated, ensuring seamless updates for users.

items.title: Launch of GPU-queue Foundation Library in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces the foundational GPU-queue library under `maestro/worker/app/queue/`, featuring a schema-agnostic Mongo queue, GPU mutual exclusion, and a watchdog for stuck processes. Comprehensive unit tests have been added to ensure reliability, and CI has been updated to include Python testing for the new components. The changes are additive, ensuring no disruption to existing functionality.

items.title: Enhance Desktop Client Authentication Flow in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This update implements a surface-aware authentication flow for the Nexior desktop client, ensuring that login functionality works seamlessly without affecting web or native behaviors. Key changes include the extraction of SSO code exchange logic, updates to the authentication panel, and the addition of localized error messages for login link expiration across 18 languages. The changes have been verified with all tests passing successfully.

items.title: Kickstarting the Electron Desktop Client in Nexior 🚀
items.summary: This PR lays the groundwork for the Nexior desktop client by introducing a dedicated surface for desktop functionality. Key additions include constants for desktop, updated utility functions to recognize the desktop environment, and a new bridge for Electron integration. All changes are additive, ensuring no impact on existing web, Android, or iOS functionalities.

items.title: Unifying GPU-Queue Architecture for Enhanced Efficiency in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update introduces a unified GPU-queue architecture that consolidates multiple projects, including maestro and digital-human, onto a single GPU. Key changes include the implementation of per-project Mongo queues, a robust kernel flock for GPU locking, and a streamlined server/worker model to maximize GPU utilization while preventing out-of-memory errors. The new design aims to enhance scalability and maintain independent project deployments.

items.title: Exciting Plans for Nexior Desktop Clients in Index 🚀
items.summary: This update outlines the plan for developing Windows and macOS desktop clients for Nexior using Electron. Key decisions include a full signed and notarized release with auto-update capabilities, and adjustments to the existing web build to accommodate desktop-specific features. The effort is estimated to take approximately two weeks for a complete release, focusing on ensuring a smooth authentication flow and compliance with platform requirements.

items.title: Boost AGENT_TIMEOUT to 90min for heavy workflows in Platform Service 🚀
items.summary: This update raises the `MAESTRO_AGENT_TIMEOUT` from 40 to 90 minutes to accommodate heavy workflows that were timing out during final assembly. Additionally, the `STALE_CLAIM_SECONDS` now derives from the `AGENT_TIMEOUT`, ensuring that stale claims exceed the timeout duration. This change is crucial for preventing finish-line timeouts while further throughput improvements are planned.

items.title: Enhance glow reduction for digital humans in Platform Service ✨
items.summary: This update introduces a stable glow reduction technique for digital humans, eliminating flicker during video processing. The new patch adjusts the per-channel mean within the face mask to match the original image, ensuring temporal stability. Additionally, the guidance scale has been lowered from 2.0 to 1.5, which helps maintain the mouth's appearance closer to the source while reducing the glow effect.
